question_text: A balloon contains helium gas at atmospheric pressure (101 kPa) with a volume of 2000 cm³. The balloon rises to a height where the atmospheric pressure drops to 81 kPa. Which statement best explains what happens to the balloon's volume?

options.label: A
options.text: The volume stays the same because helium is lighter than air.

options.label: B
options.text: The volume increases to approximately 2500 cm³ because the external pressure decreases.

options.label: C
options.text: The volume decreases because helium expands when it gets colder.

options.label: D
options.text: The volume increases to 5000 cm³ because the pressure doubles.

explanation: Using Boyle's law, when external pressure decreases from 101 kPa to 81 kPa, the gas expands. Calculating: 101 × 2000 = 81 × V₂, so V₂ = 202000 ÷ 81 ≈ 2494 cm³ (approximately 2500 cm³). The balloon expands because there is less external pressure pushing inward on it.
